title: Put cost guardrails on data transfer, NAT, and region choice
description: Budget the silent cost drivers (NAT data processing, cross-AZ/cross-region transfer, idle provisioned capacity) and pin region/AZ topology to cut egress.
body: Use when designing topology or investigating an AWS bill. Do not route all private traffic through a single NAT Gateway by reflex and avoid cross-region calls on a hot path. Actions: add AWS Budgets with alerts, replace NAT egress to AWS services with VPC Gateway/Interface endpoints, keep chatty components in the same AZ to dodge cross-AZ charges, and review Cost Explorer grouped by usage type. Validation signals include Cost Explorer showing reduced NAT DataProcessing-Bytes, lower DataTransfer-Regional-Bytes, and a budget alarm that fires before overspend. Risk boundary: a topology change that moves production traffic across AZ/region boundaries is human-gated.
